people_highlights.name: Sam Altman
people_highlights.post:
i keep thinking i want the models to be cheaper/faster more than i want them to be smarter

but it seems that just being smarter is still the most important thing

people_highlights.name: François Chollet
people_highlights.post:
I wrote Deep Learning with Python to be the definitive guide to how deep learning works and how to best make use of it. Tens of thousands of people got their career start via this book. 120,000 copies sold, and downloaded by millions more.

And now it's free to read online:

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post:
Sometimes when I demo AI, I show it turning cover letters into goofy formats (poetry, etc) as an introduction to the idea of AI as translator between forms.

For the first time, GPT-5.5 has been trying to get me to tone these requests down so I don’t ruin my chances at the job.

people_highlights.name: Swami Sivasubramanian
people_highlights.post: Agents that perform well at launch don't often stay that way. Models evolve, user behavior shifts, and quality quietly degrades in ways that are hard to spot and harder to fix. Most teams are still relying on manual cycles of reading traces, guessing fixes, and deploying without
